headline: Amazon Outage Forces Hundreds of Websites Offline for Hours
snippet: Amazon Web Services, a major provider of cloud services, cited a problem at its data center in Northern Virginia. The outage highlighted the fragility of global internet infrastructure.

headline: How Chile Embodies A.I.’s No-Win Politics
snippet: Political debates have flared across Chile over artificial intelligence. Should the nation pour billions into A.I. and risk public backlash, or risk being left behind?
byline: By Paul Mozur and Marcos Zegers

headline: What Is Amazon Web Services?
snippet: The cloud-computing division of the internet giant is used by thousands of internet customers, many of whom reported disruptions on Monday.

headline: The Showdown Behind the Shutdown
snippet: Extending subsidies for the Affordable Care Act has emerged as a major sticking point in Washington, and could carry the biggest economic consequences.
byline: By Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly and Ian Mount

headline: Appeals Court Lifted a Block on Trump’s Deployment to Oregon
snippet: Also, the search is on for the Louvre jewel thieves. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.

headline: The Louvre Heist
snippet: Today, we look at how thieves pulled off this weekend’s brazen daylight robbery in Paris and why they did it.

headline: What Could Torpedo the Stock Rally
snippet: The S&P 500 is nearing another record, but some analysts caution that a host of factors may stall its momentum.
byline: By Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced and Niko Gallogly

headline: U.S. Health Workers Recalled From Shutdown Furlough for Medicare, A.C.A. Enrollments
snippet: An estimated 3,000 employees seem to be affected. The federal agency said the return to work would occur Monday, with the costs paid by user fees collected from researchers.

headline: Smart Beds Helped Them Sleep on a Cloud. Then the Cloud Crashed.
snippet: The widespread outage involving the cloud-computing provider Amazon Web Services ensnared unexpected consumers earlier this week: people who just wanted a good night’s sleep.

headline: The U.S. Is Deploying an Aircraft Carrier to Latin America
snippet: Also, the World Series begins. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.

headline: Starring in Videos Is No Longer a Job Just for the Social Media Team
snippet: Companies from Delta Air Lines to Portillo’s are asking all sorts of employees, sometimes through formal programs, to help build the brand.

headline: Big Tech Makes Cal State Its A.I. Training Ground
snippet: Spurred by titans like Amazon and OpenAI, California State wants to become the nation’s “largest A.I.-empowered” university.
byline: By Natasha Singer and Philip Cheung

headline: Amazon’s Profit Is Up 38% on Strong Performance
snippet: After unexpectedly strong sales and profits across its consumer and cloud businesses, the tech giant said another strong quarter might be ahead.

headline: Amazon’s Big Spending Reignites an A.I. Stock Rally
snippet: Investors cheered the tech giant’s latest results showing that its huge investments in artificial intelligence are beginning to show returns.
byline: By Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Grady McGregor, Ian Mount and Lauren Hirsch

headline: We Now Have a Better Picture of China’s Global Lending Spree
snippet: Beijing has used loans to developing nations to expand its influence, but a new study says no country has received more Chinese financing than the United States.

headline: Cloudflare Says It Has Resolved Outage That Disrupted Parts of the Internet
snippet: Services from Cloudflare, a software company, underpin thousands of websites, including X, Spotify and OpenAI. The company said a crash in a software system was to blame.
